citations.narrative:
The enclosure for two white geese had an electrical plug with an approximately eight-inch extension that had frayed and
exposed non-insulated electrical wires. The damaged wiring was located approximately one foot above the ground.
Possible contact with exposed electrical wiring could cause injury to the animals.
Housing facilities for birds must be designed and constructed so that they are structurally sound for the species of bird
housed in them. They must be kept in good repair, protect the birds from injury, and restrict other animals from entering
that may negatively affect the welfare of the birds within.
Corrected at time of inspection.

citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
The facility had not observed that a six -year -old male pot -bellied pig named “Pig Pig” had an overgrown left tusk that
had penetrated the skin of the facial area below the eye. A yellow creamy substance was draining from the facial wound.
The tips of both ears had an approximately four-inch-long lesion that was open, red, hairless, and swollen. The attending
veterinarian was not contacted about health issues. Open wounds if left untreated may lead to more serious infections
and serious health issues.
Each dealer or exhibitor shall establish and maintain programs of adequate veterinary care that include: Daily observation
of all animals to assess their health and well-being; Provided, however, That daily observation of animals may be
accomplished by someone other than the attending veterinarian; and Provided, further, That a mechanism of direct and
frequent communication is required so that timely and accurate information on problems of animal health, behavior, and
well-being is conveyed to the attending veterinarian.
The facility contacted the attending veterinarian and provided immediate treatment for the conditions.

In an indoor building, a primary enclosure is currently housing five weaned cats. The enclosure does not contain an
elevated resting surface. All cat enclosures must have elevated resting surfaces large enough to safely contain all animals
in the enclosure. The facility must provide adequate resting surfaces for all cats.
Corrected at time of inspection.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
n

narrative:
3.150(a) 
   
Facilities, general. 
The enclosure for two white geese had an electrical plug with an approximately eight-inch extension that had frayed and 
exposed non-insulated electrical wires. The damaged wiring was located approximately one foot above the ground. 
Possible contact with exposed electrical wiring could cause injury to the animals. 
 
Housing facilities for birds must be designed and constructed so that they are structurally sound for the species of bird 
housed in them. They must be kept in good repair, protect the birds from injury, and restrict other animals from entering 
that may negatively affect the welfare of the birds within. 
 
Corrected at time of inspection. 
2.40(b)(3)  Direct Repeat
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
The facility had not observed that a six -year -old male pot -bellied pig named “Pig Pig” had an overgrown left tusk that 
had penetrated the skin of the facial area below the eye. A yellow creamy substance was draining from the facial wound. 
The tips of both ears had an approximately four-inch-long lesion that was open, red, hairless, and swollen. The attending 
veterinarian was not contacted about health issues. Open wounds if left untreated may lead to more serious infections 
and serious health issues.  
 
Each dealer or exhibitor shall establish and maintain programs of adequate veterinary care that include: Daily observation 
of all animals to assess their health and well-being; Provided, however, That daily observation of animals may be 
accomplished by someone other than the attending veterinarian; and Provided, further, That a mechanism of direct and 
frequent communication is required so that timely and accurate information on problems of animal health, behavior, and 
well-being is conveyed to the attending veterinarian.  
 
The facility contacted the attending veterinarian and provided immediate treatment for the conditions. 
3.6(b)(4)  Repeat

Primary enclosures. 
In an indoor building, a primary enclosure is currently housing five weaned cats. The enclosure does not contain an 
elevated resting surface. All cat enclosures must have elevated resting surfaces large enough to safely contain all animals 
in the enclosure. The facility must provide adequate resting surfaces for all cats. 
 
Corrected at time of inspection. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
